title: cerastium alpinum var. squalidum
intro: Cerastium alpinum var. squalidum, also known as Mountain Chickweed or Alpine Whitlow Grass, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum var. squalidum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into a salve or ointment, which can be applied topically to affected areas to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
